title: Citharexylum glabrum
taxonomy: Citharexylum glabrum is a species of flowering plant in the family Verbenaceae. This family is commonly known as the verbena or jasmine family. The Verbenaceae family is part of the larger clade asterids, which is a group of flowering plants. Citharexylum glabrum is also known as the glabrous fiddlewood or simply fiddlewood. It is native to Central America and southern Mexico, where it thrives in tropical and subtropical environments.
uses: Citharexylum glabrum is a plant species that has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. intro: Citharexylum glabrum, also known as "Cabezon," is a shrub-like plant native to tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, Citharexylum glabrum is used in herbal medicine to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Citharexylum glabrum is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
